stem: A manufacturing study measures defect rate across 4 factorys, each containing multiple production lines. factory A has 10 observations with mean 69.7 % (within-factory SD = 7.5). factory B has 8 observations with mean 56.7 % (within-factory SD = 8.9). factory C has 10 observations with mean 66.7 % (within-factory SD = 8.0). factory D has 5 observations with mean 60.1 % (within-factory SD = 7.8). The between-factory standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 6.8 and 17.1 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true defect rate for factory D, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-factory variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for factory D: 60.5 % (shrunk from raw 60.1). Range [60.3, 61.0] as between-factory SD varies from 6.8 to 17.1. Pooled mean: 64.2.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 64.2 % (weighted average of 4 factory means).
Shrinkage factor for factory D: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/5). At σ_between=11.4: B = 0.914.
Interval [60.3, 61.0] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (60.1) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (64.2) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
